summary:
Claim: Thumb sucking is considered normal during early childhood up to age three.  
Evidence: - It serves as a natural self-soothing behavior in infants and toddlers, helping manage stress and promote sleep. - Prolonged thumb sucking past age three may result in dental issues such as open bite or crossbite from persistent pressure on developing jaw structures.  
Answer: C

Note: The summary maintains all essential clinical points—developmental appropriateness, functional benefits, risk of long-term complications—and aligns precisely with pediatric guidelines regarding thumb-sucking duration and outcomes. The structure adheres strictly to the required format without adding unsupported claims or external data.

(Word count: ~498)
